subject: general
title: Managing 47 students alone — it becomes chaotic
query_text: Managing 47 students alone — it becomes chaotic

immediate_steps.title: Immediate Action
steps: Divide your 47 students into 5 fixed groups of 9-10. Give each group a name (colours, animals). Groups stay the same every day — this alone reduces chaos significantly.
Appoint one 'Group Leader' per group. This student is your helper — distributes materials, keeps noise check, reports issues. Rotate leaders weekly so everyone gets a turn.
Use a simple signal system: one clap from you = freeze and look at you. Practise this signal for 2 minutes at the start of class until it becomes automatic.

classroom_activity.title: Classroom Activity — Group Survey (EVS/General)
classroom_activity.activity: While you work with one group directly, other groups do a structured peer task: each group surveys their own members — 'What is one thing you like about our class? What is one thing we can improve?' The Group Leader collects answers on a slate or scrap paper. After 10 minutes, one leader from each group shares findings. This keeps all 47 students purposefully engaged without needing your constant attention.
classroom_activity.materials: Slates, chalk, or any scrap paper already in classroom

inclusion_strategy.title: Inclusion Strategy
inclusion_strategy.strategy: Place one stronger reader and one quieter or struggling student in each group intentionally. The peer-teaching that naturally happens reduces your load and builds confidence in both students — the one explaining learns more deeply, and the struggling learner feels less exposed.

reflection_prompt.title: Reflection
reflection_prompt.question: After trying the fixed groups and signal system for one week, which part of your class felt calmer — and what made that possible?

source_citation.title: Source
source_citation.reference: TaRL (Teaching at the Right Level) Methodology — Pratham: grouped learning structures for large mixed-ability classrooms; NCERT Class-5 EVS 'Our Wondrous World' — small group discussion and peer survey activity (Activity 6 & 9)
